coachNote: Today is a full baseline session. Warm up on C and G, then the real work — the black-key scales hands separate, then together. Two minor scales are new (your old plan couldn’t even show them). Finish with the chord shapes and pop progressions that make songs readable. Rate each one honestly — that’s what lets me shape tomorrow around you.

exercises.label: C major — hands together
exercises.cue: One octave up and down, comfortable tempo. Just wake the hands up.

exercises.label: G major — hands together
exercises.cue: Same home shape, hands together. Loose and even.

exercises.label: B♭ major — right hand
exercises.cue: Right hand alone, slowly. Start on a long finger; the thumb only lands on a white key.

exercises.label: B♭ major — left hand
exercises.cue: Left hand alone, slowly. Lead with the 1→4 pivot: thumb on a white key, then 4 reaches over.

exercises.label: B♭ major — hands together
exercises.cue: Both hands, as slow as it takes to stay clean. Slip? Stop, fix that one crossing, carry on.

exercises.label: E♭ major — right hand
exercises.cue: Right hand alone, slowly. Start on a long finger; the thumb only lands on a white key.

exercises.label: E♭ major — left hand
exercises.cue: Left hand alone, slowly. Lead with the 1→4 pivot: thumb on a white key, then 4 reaches over.

exercises.label: E♭ major — hands together
exercises.cue: Both hands, as slow as it takes to stay clean. Slip? Stop, fix that one crossing, carry on.

exercises.label: G♭ major — right hand
exercises.cue: Right hand alone, slowly. Start on a long finger; the thumb only lands on a white key.

exercises.label: G♭ major — left hand
exercises.cue: Left hand alone, slowly. Lead with the 1→4 pivot: thumb on a white key, then 4 reaches over.

exercises.label: G♭ major — hands together
exercises.cue: Both hands, as slow as it takes to stay clean. Slip? Stop, fix that one crossing, carry on.

exercises.label: A natural minor — right hand
exercises.cue: New today. Same fingering as C major — natural minor is the white keys from A. Hear the darker color.

exercises.label: A natural minor — left hand
exercises.cue: Left hand alone. Same home LH shape, starting on A.

exercises.label: E natural minor — right hand
exercises.cue: E minor, right hand. One sharp (F♯) — the key signature handles it; finger it like C major.

exercises.label: C major I–IV–V triads — hands together
exercises.abc:
X:1
M:4/4
L:1/1
K:C
V:1 clef=treble
V:2 clef=bass
[V:1] "C"[CEG] | "F"[FAc] | "G"[GBd] |]
[V:2] C, | F, | G, |]
exercises.cue: Block each triad, hands together. Let the chord drop from the arm; don’t press.

exercises.label: C major inversions — root, 1st, 2nd
exercises.fingering: RH 1 3 5 on each shape · feel how the hand shifts up
exercises.cue: Same three notes, repackaged. Name each shape out loud — this is how you read slash chords on a lead sheet.

exercises.label: I–V–vi–IV in C — the four-chord loop
exercises.fingering: RH 1 3 5 throughout · LH plays the single root under each
exercises.cue: The pop loop behind hundreds of songs. Smooth, even pulse; add the left-hand root when it feels easy.

exercises.label: ii–V–I in C — the turnaround
exercises.fingering: RH 1 3 5 on each · the move that ends a thousand songs
exercises.cue: Listen for the pull of G back home to C. This three-chord cadence is everywhere once you hear it.
